name: nizam-governance
description: Unified, runtime-agnostic capability manifest for the Nizam governance framework (DD-4): a single skill payload any agent runtime can load to plan, execute, gate, and durably record contract-first work.

capabilities.name: planning
capabilities.description: Planning enforcement: the mandatory product_spec.md + feature_list.json artifact pair, the feature list as a validated dependency graph, and the Dependency Enforcement Rule.

capabilities.name: contract_first_execution
capabilities.description: The two-loop state machine (pre-code contract alignment, post-code implementation repair) and the JSON Verdict Parse Rule gating every stage.

capabilities.name: adversarial_testing
capabilities.description: Evaluator independence rules, false-pass and false-fail hunting patterns, and the mandatory per-round adversarial spot-check.

capabilities.name: circuit_breaker
capabilities.description: The universal 3-strike attempt limit embedded by every repeatable execution loop, its per-attempt strategy table, and the forbidden-fourth-attempt halt/escalation procedure.

capabilities.name: durable_state
capabilities.description: Query-the-index (not bulk-read) discipline for locating governance files, evidence externalisation to file paths, and the durable-state artifact families every run maintains.

capabilities.name: release_train
capabilities.description: Semantic-version git-tag release gates, changelog discipline, and the consumer upgrade path via re-bootstrap against a new pinned tag.

capabilities.name: documentation_standard
capabilities.description: The required frontmatter keys and file-lifecycle rules every governed document must satisfy.

capabilities.name: governance_inheritance
capabilities.description: How a consumer repository inherits this framework's governance content at bootstrap time.

capabilities.name: agent_governance
capabilities.description: The four agent roles, the dual validator gate, the JSON verdict parse rule, and the durable-state (no oral tradition) rule.

capabilities.name: anti_hallucination
capabilities.description: The mandatory constraints preventing hallucinated edits, phantom fixes, and unsubstantiated completion claims.

capabilities.name: ecosystem_bootstrap
capabilities.description: The ecosystem cycle's entry stage: how a consumer repository adopts the framework from a pinned, immutable released tag (via the Governance Inheritance Protocol and bootstrap.sh), verifies the injected payload, and records its provenance pin before any later stage may run.

capabilities.name: ecosystem_preflight
capabilities.description: Clean-state preflight gating entry into the ecosystem cycle: three-verdict vocabulary and the operator-exception rule for PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS.

capabilities.name: ecosystem_baseline
capabilities.description: Capturing a point-in-time, immutable baseline over framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, and evidence references, every fact anchored to a stated revision and timestamp.

capabilities.name: ecosystem_audit
capabilities.description: Evidence-first engineering audit against the ten-state maturity model and finding-confidence vocabulary, excluding commercial readiness from generic engineering scoring.

capabilities.name: ecosystem_dependency_reconciliation
capabilities.description: The Plan stage: turning approved audit findings plus the ecosystem-level membership-run aggregate into typed, dependency-ordered cross-repository work packets under the topological-order invariant (a cyclic set forces a non-PASS plan), gated by the H-PLANNING-AUTHORITY operator decision.

capabilities.name: ecosystem_progress_comparison
capabilities.description: Comparing two approved baselines or audits, classifying new/resolved/reopened/stale findings with every score movement traceable to evidence.

file_read_write.description: The host environment must be able to read and write files in the consumer repository, including a durable-state directory (conventionally .agent/).

shell_execution.description: The host environment must be able to run verification commands (tests, checks, schema validation) and capture their exit codes and output.

structured_data_parsing.description: The host environment must be able to parse and emit JSON, and parse YAML frontmatter blocks and YAML phase/template documents.

root_index.description: The compact root capability index and context router an agent queries first (DD-1) to locate the minimal set of governance files a task requires.
